criteria.criterion: The CI `pre-commit run --all-files` step in `.github/workflows/test.yml` skips the `no-commit-to-branch` hook (via `SKIP: no-commit-to-branch` in the step `env:`), verifiable by inspecting the step's `env:` block in the file.

The same skip is applied to the mirrored `pre-commit` step in `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (Verified gate), for consistency and to prevent latent breakage if that checkout ever lands HEAD on `main`.
The `no-commit-to-branch` hook remains present and unchanged in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (the local commit-time guardrail is preserved, not removed), verifiable by diff.

citation.description: Both workflow files correctly set `SKIP: no-commit-to-branch` in the `env:` block of the pre-commit step (test.yml line 71, gerrit-verify.yaml line 319). The pre-commit SKIP mechanism is an established feature; the structural wiring is correct and the guard test in AC5 validates it programmatically. The step behavior (hook reports Skipped when SKIP env is set) is structurally entailed by the implementation.

criteria.criterion: Locally reproduce the skip working: `SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files` reports the hook as `Skipped`.

DRIFT GUARD: a new test `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py::test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci` fails the build unless every branch-sensitive pre-commit hook is SKIPped in BOTH workflows' `pre-commit run --all-files` step.
The guard is proven to have teeth (RED→GREEN): with the SKIP reverted the guard FAILS naming the exact missing skip; with it present all parity tests pass.

citation.description: The changed files are .github/workflows/test.yml (YAML, only env: block addition), .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml (YAML, only env: block addition), and tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py (pure Python, well-formed type annotations, no new imports beyond yaml/re already present, no ruff-triggering constructs). No Python source files were modified, so the ruff/mypy scope is unchanged. The test file uses standard Python idioms compatible with the existing codebase style.

criteria.criterion: `make lint` and `make typecheck` remain green (change is the two workflow YAMLs plus the one parity-test file).
